_comment: CAST Universal Design for Learning (UDL) Guidelines 3.0 (July 2024). 3 principles, 9 guidelines, 31 checkpoints. Used by udl-options and accommodation-match atoms. Source: udlguidelines.cast.org. Licensed under Creative Commons BY-SA 4.0.
source: CAST (2024). Universal Design for Learning Guidelines version 3.0. https://udlguidelines.cast.org
goal: Develop expert learners who are purposeful and motivated, resourceful and knowledgeable, and strategic and goal-directed.

principles.description: The WHY of learning. Provide multiple means of engagement to tap into learners' interests, challenge them appropriately, and motivate them.

checkpoints.text: Heighten salience of goals and objectives
Vary demands and resources to optimize challenge
Promote expectations and beliefs that optimize motivation
Facilitate personal coping skills and strategies

principles.description: The WHAT of learning. Provide multiple means of representation to give learners various ways of acquiring information and knowledge.

checkpoints.text: Offer ways of customizing the display of information
Support decoding of text, mathematical notation, and symbols
Highlight patterns, critical features, big ideas, and relationships

principles.description: The HOW of learning. Provide multiple means of action and expression to allow learners alternatives for demonstrating what they know.

checkpoints.text: Vary the methods for response and navigation
Optimize access to tools and assistive technologies
Use multiple tools for construction and composition
Build fluencies with graduated levels of support for practice and performance
